item.title: PBS dispute could leave MS patients with huge bills for crucial drugs Ocrevus and Kesimpta
item.description: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.

item.title: Cancer survivor Torstein Træen claims Tour de France yellow jersey on desperately hot stage four
item.description: Torstein Træen was diagnosed with testicular cancer in 2022, leading to the removal of his left testicle. Three years later, he is the overall race leader at the Tour de France.

item.title: China missile launch tests Pacific relations as Australia looks to pounce
item.description: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

item.title: Oral care giant Hismile used 'misleading' videos in social media advertisements
item.description: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

item.title: Shopping seasonally this July looks like ...
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Is AI changing your life in unexpected ways? Should we move faster or more cautiously? The ABC wants to hear from you
item.description: Most of us never chose to live in an artificial intelligence (AI) world. Yet over the past few years, it has become part of everyday life, changing how we think, learn and communicate.

item.title: Australian woman arrested in US after allegedly voting illegally in federal elections
item.description: An Australian woman has been arrested in the United States after an ICE investigation allegedly found she had illegally voted in multiple federal elections.

item.title: Australia issued blunt warning over AUKUS and China's nuclear capabilities
item.description: Victor Gao, the vice-president of a major Chinese think tank, conducts some sabre-rattling in the wake of China's missile test in the Pacific.

item.title: Report shows ambulance call-outs plummet during initial years of Richmond's medically supervised injecting room
item.description: Data from a new paper shows the number of heroin-related ambulance call-outs dropped from 48 a month to 14 a month in the first five years following the Richmond medically supervised injecting room's opening.

item.title: Birds in Melbourne's west have 'highly elevated' PFAS in their blood, study finds
item.description: Blood samples found levels of the "forever chemicals" in sparrows near contaminated industrial and military sites were 10 times higher than those in a comparison population.

item.title: Fears for jobs as SunRice cuts shifts due to drought and water buybacks
item.description: Communities in southern NSW fear large cutbacks to rice processing could put hundreds of people out of work and decimate regional economies.

item.title: Angus Taylor backs shadow minister for test-calling Triple Zero — as it happened
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has backed Sarah Henderson's decision to test-call Triple Zero, saying she was "doing her job".

item.title: SA police investigating possible Telstra outage death — as it happened
item.description: South Australian police confirm they are investigating a death possibly linked to Telstra outage. Look back at the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News markets blog.

item.title: Ukraine's Kostyuk extends Wimbledon run and slams IOC decision on Russia
item.description: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk is through to the Wimbledon semifinals. The 24-year-old says she finds it "tough" as her hometown of Kyiv continues to come under attack from Russia.

item.title: Neil the seal 'may have moved on', Tasmanian wildlife experts say
item.description: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

item.title: I'd always wanted to carry a baby. Cancer meant I couldn't
item.description: The grief of losing the chance to have baby is something I expect to carry forever. But I'm becoming more comfortable with the unconventional life I'm building with my partner April.

item.title: NT Police charge 34yo Jordan Garrawurra with murder after 17yo girl found dead in Galiwin'ku home
item.description: Days after a 17-year-old girl was found dead inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, police have charged a 34-year-old man following an autopsy.

item.title: State of Origin Game III the highest-rating program in series history
item.description: An unprecedented number of viewers tuned into the State of Origin decider, in which NSW stunned Queensland to win the series.

item.title: Angus Taylor says One Nation policies would bring 'eternity of pain' to economy
item.description: In his strongest remarks pushing back on One Nation, the opposition leader has accused the right-wing party of being a "column of smoke".

item.title: Three NT corrections officers stood down with pay over prisoner escape
item.description: The Northern Territory Corrections Department has stood down three of its workers with pay over a prisoner escape on Sunday that triggered a more than 48-hour manhunt.

item.title: Narendra Modi given rapturous welcome as he addresses thousands at community event in Melbourne
item.description: More than 20,000 people have given a rapturous welcome to Narendra Modi at Melbourne's Docklands Stadium as Anthony Albanese heaps praise on Australia's Indian diaspora.

item.title: How Beijing 'tripled the size of its nuclear arsenal' in six years
item.description: China's firing of a dummy warhead was the first time in two years it tested its long-range missile arsenal — and the first time ever it had launched such a rocket from a nuclear-powered submarine.

item.title: Climate change becoming a major driver of child marriage across Asia and the Pacific
item.description: Climate change is now believed to be a leading contributor to more frequent and younger nuptials, with 12 million girls tipped to become child brides this year.

item.title: AI, gambling and Middle East set for Labor conference debate
item.description: A draft document, seen by ABC, reveals AI, gambling and the Middle East are expected to feature heavily in Labor's upcoming national conference debate.

item.title: The joy of Neil: How a cheeky seal on Tasmania’s shores brought happiness to the world
item.description: Neil the seal has left his resting spot in Tasmania, leaving a wildly popular social media catalogue of bollard-bashing, fence-crushing videos.

item.title: Aussie Rules club Fregon Bulldogs' new guernseys bring change in fortunes
item.description: The Fregon Bulldogs are among the nation's most remote Australian Rules football clubs, and for several seasons were forced to play in casual clothes. But the club has secured new guernseys — and the change in uniform has led to a change in form.

item.title: Australian War Memorial makes 'magnificent' discovery of first Indigenous Military Cross recipient
item.description: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

item.title: The meteorite at the centre of a 'culture war'
item.description: The fight to claim a 3.5-tonne space rock didn't just kickstart a publicity battle that divided an empire — it also left a gaping hole in culture. Centuries on, the question remains: Who owned it?

item.title: Hanson doubles down on call for Coalition to work with One Nation — as it happened
item.description: Pauline Hanson has doubled down on calls for the Coalition to work with One Nation.

item.title: ASX closes the week higher after Telstra told to 'face the music' over outage — As it happened
item.description: The Australian share market closed higher on Friday after the latest escalation of tensions in the Middle East sent oil prices higher. Telstra faced further pressure over the national network outage.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: Karolina Muchová and Linda Nosková set up all-Czech final as Coco Gauff misses golden chance
item.description: Coco Gauff is left to rue a regulation miss on match point as Karolina Muchová and Linda Nosková set up an all-Czech date at Wimbledon.

item.title: Judge hands down verdict in Jayson Gillham and Melbourne Symphony Orchestra trial
item.description: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

item.title: Roundabout to be built at Bruce Highway–Walker St intersection in Maryborough
item.description: The Queensland government will build a four-lane roundabout at a Bruce Highway intersection that claimed three lives.

item.title: Why potatoes can be the star of the show and what types to use
item.description: Potatoes are undeniably versatile and tasty but choosing which variety to buy has never been more complicated. Brushed or washed? Yellow, white or pink? Mashing or roasting? And now there are low-carb spuds too.

item.title: BBL to play first overseas match in India as Scorchers face Renegades
item.description: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

item.title: Claim woman's death caused by Telstra outage is 'incorrect', SA Police say
item.description: South Australia's police chief says there is "nothing to suggest" the death of a person in a regional hospital was caused by any failure to connect to Triple Zero (000) during the Telstra outage, following an investigation into the matter.

item.title: World Cup: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win in any fashion
item.description: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

item.title: Six-year-old boy dies after two-vehicle crash at Gleneagle in the Scenic Rim
item.description: A six-year-old boy has died in hospital following a two-vehicle crash near Beaudesert on Monday.

item.title: Wallabies captain says recent results not good enough ahead of Test match against France
item.description: Close enough will not be good enough for the Wallabies, even with an untried flyhalf and familiar French pack ready to inflict a sixth-straight Test defeat on the hosts in Brisbane.

item.title: Professor Clemente explains insights from spider racing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The ASX finished trading moderately higher on reports the US and Iran will meet for "technical talks".
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Pies coach says AFL review technology 'not up to scratch' after thriller
item.description: Craig McRae takes aim at the AFL's ARC after Collingwood's thrilling win over North Melbourne.

item.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket recovery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: China achieves first-ever controlled recovery of orbital-class rocket
item.description: China has successfully tested an experimental rocket retrieval system, state media reported, in the hope of breaking US dominance in reusable rockets.

item.title: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell in collision involving pedestrian
item.description: Police are investigating the collision which took place on Port Road at Woodville on Friday morning.

item.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up new art gallery in Bangkok, Thailand
item.description: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

item.title: Cold Chisel saxophonist Andy Bickers dies from cancer, tributes paid to 'incredible musician'
item.description: Cold Chisel saxophone player Andy Bickers has died after "hard fought battle with cancer", the band has announced.

item.title: All-star cast set sail in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey
item.description: Christopher Nolan and the cast of the highly anticipated The Odyssey sit down with ABC News Breakfast to talk about the upcoming film.

item.title: The battle for the Blue Zones
item.description: The Blue Zones grew into a global wellness brand aided by stories of legendary old age. But a bitter feud over the science has shaken their foundations.

item.title: BYD backflips and offers full refunds after customers given cars from wrong year
item.description: More than 1,200 customers who bought electric and hybrid cars from Chinese maker BYD will be offered a full refund after the company admitted to selling them vehicles from the wrong year.

item.title: NATO was a lesson in how the geopolitical landscape has shifted around Trump
item.description: Trump demanded to be the centre of attention. But the more significant news out of NATO was really about everyone else.

item.title: Curbing well-off taxpayers' access to key programs could save billions
item.description: Curbing access to the child care subsidy, parental leave pay, aged care and pensions for some of the most well-off taxpayers would free up about $21 billion a year, a new report says.

item.title: Cruz Hewitt becomes first Australian since Alex de Minaur to reach Wimbledon boys' final
item.description: Cruz Hewitt continues his dominant run at Wimbledon, keeping his straight sets record intact en-route to the boys' final.

item.title: H5 bird flu poses risk to SA's Coorong critical wetlands
item.description: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

item.title: Living in the 'forgotten' community of Beagle Bay
item.description: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

item.title: Broken Hill mother exposed to lead as a child fights same battle with her children
item.description: Baylee Stokes lives with the health impacts of being exposed to lead as a child. Now, she's considering moving away from her hometown to protect her own children.

item.title: Remembering the quadriplegic artist who drew the outback without using his hands
item.description: Left quadriplegic in an accident, every line the late Gunggari man drew and every word he scrawled in ink was created without a pen in his hands.

item.title: Hepburn Shire mayor says charges in 'private prosecution' by resident a 'threat to democracy'
item.description: The mayor of Hepburn Shire in Victoria is the third council representative to be charged by the same resident via a rare "private prosecution".

item.title: Carbon monoxide poisoning suspected in fatal Mount Gambier incident
item.description: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

item.title: Hardwick lashes 'peanut' football fans after fiery run-in with spectator
item.description: Damien Hardwick clashes with Crows fans at Adelaide Oval, as Jeremy Cameron's injury timeline is revealed.

item.title: Meta pulls new Instagram AI feature after widespread backlash
item.description: The automatic feature, integrated into its Meta AI chatbot, let users edit generated images directly through sketches.

item.title: Man, 74, critical after being assaulted on early morning walk in Yennora
item.description: A 74-year-old man is in a critical condition after being assaulted in Western Sydney.

item.title: 'Lost feeling down one arm': Dogs coach reveals extent of Crichton injury
item.description: Canterbury captain Stephen Crichton is removed from the the Bulldogs' 40-16 loss to the Raiders on a medicab, with coach Cameron Ciraldo confirming a recurrence of nerve issues in his neck and shoulder.

item.title: Telstra will face Senate inquiry after nationwide outage
item.description: The telco will face a grilling from politicians about this week's nationwide outage that brought businesses and transport systems into chaos.

item.title: Police lockdown of Emu Park neighbourhood lifted
item.description: Police declared a public emergency after shots were reportedly fired at a property in Emu Park, central Queensland.

item.title: France dominates Wallabies to post historic Test win
item.description: Following a second-half blitz, France scores its fourth straight victory over the Wallabies, triumphing 42-26 in the Nations Championship Test in Brisbane.

item.title: Speedboat capsize off Vietnam's Phu Quoc island kills 15 tourists
item.description: A tourist boat returning from an island trip in southern Vietnam has capsized, killing 15 Indian visitors, state media has reported.

item.title: Can Australia avoid the next round of the global energy crisis?
item.description: As the conflict has dragged on, gas prices have soared and now threaten to up-end the economies of Europe and Asia.

item.title: Australia's first map of city zoning shows Melbourne streets ahead on density
item.description: Four years on from a national pledge to unlock more housing, only one capital city allows townhouses or three-storey developments on most of its residential land.

item.title: Telstra outage fallout will put overseas holidays on the backburner for anxious leaders
item.description: For Australian politicians, Scott Morrison's ill-fated Hawaii vacation in December 2019 remains a potent reminder of what not to do in a national crisis.

item.title: Linda Nosková survives Karolína Muchová fightback in all-Czech final to lift Wimbledon women's singles trophy
item.description: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.

item.title: Socceroos star Jordy Bos sidelined for months after undergoing successful knee surgery
item.description: Jordy Bos goes under the knife after his World Cup campaign came to an abrupt end due to a knee injury against Egypt.

item.title: Invisible string that Australia attached to PNG's rugby league team
item.description: In a contest for Pacific influence against China, Australia's football diplomacy comes with risks and rewards.

item.title: Ensay's football homecoming revives decades-old legacy
item.description: A special tribute match at Ensay's oval in regional Victoria resurrected a football tradition and brought generations of a Gippsland high country community together.

item.title: Artist Julie Aldridge's 10-year battle to reclaim mosaic design from the internet
item.description: A South Australian artist is fighting drop-ship company Temu over its selling of her mosaic design on rugs. But how did the artwork get there in the first place?

item.title: How a fireman helped make dinosaur fossils cool again
item.description: Travis Enright is four months into investigating what he calls "a 100-million-year-old cold case" involving a 10-metre-long aquatic dinosaur called Moria.

item.title: Horsham footballer 'alert' in Melbourne hospital after on-field head knock
item.description: A young regional footballer who sustained a head knock and concussion during a match on Saturday is recovering well in a Melbourne hospital, according to his club.

item.title: China ballistic missile test pushes forward case for Pacific Security Agreement
item.description: Pacific Minister Pat Conroy says the testing of a nuclear-capable missile damaged China's reputation and has strengthened the case for a Pacific security pact.

item.title: World Cup controversy as Norway fumes over England's spidercam-assisted equaliser
item.description: Norway felt England's equaliser shouldn't have stood after the ball appeared to get a deflection off a wire holding up the "spidercam" above the pitch, directly leading to England gaining possession and scoring.

item.title: World Cup: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road
item.description: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

item.title: WA Premier Roger Cook takes on defence industries and tourism in cabinet reshuffle
item.description: WA Premier Roger Cook will take on the defence industries and tourism portfolios in a cabinet reshuffle, following the departure of longstanding minister Paul Papalia.

item.title: Can the pill affect your mental health? Young woman says mood swings were too much to bear
item.description: Two young women say taking the pill to manage painful periods had side effects they weren't expecting.

item.title: World Cup: Switzerland blasts 'unacceptable' VAR rule after 3-1 loss to Argentina
item.description: Switzerland is fuming after Breel Embolo was sent of the highly charged World Cup quarterfinal against Argentina in Kansas City.

item.title: Jai Arrow vows to fight MND for everyone during 'World's Biggest Birthday Party'
item.description: Former NRL player Jai Arrow says he is determined to raise money and awareness for Motor Neurone Disease until a cure is found.

item.title: Victim of antisemitic attack wants graffiti to remain to show family was targeted
item.description: The victim of an antisemitic attack says he doesn't want to clean up offensive graffiti sprayed on his Adelaide property, because he wants the public to see his family was targeted

item.title: Pharmacy lobby preventing Australians from buying cheaper medicines, Grattan Institute says
item.description: Taxpayers are missing out on cheaper medicines due to backroom deals by one of Australia's most powerful lobby groups, according to a new report.

item.title: Universities required to define antisemitism under new racism standards
item.description: The new standards will require institutions to adopt definitions of antisemitism, racism against Aboriginal and Torres Straight Islander peoples, and Islamophobia.

item.title: Small and achievable ways to drink less alcohol
item.description: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

item.title: With AI, we have returned to a world of two competing superpowers
item.description: When it comes to AI, Australia is a spectator at a UFC cage fight between the United States and China, except the spectators are inside the cage with the combatants.

item.title: Labor scoring own goals while Coalition distracted by One Nation threat
item.description: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

item.title: As swift parrots kept breeding in this forest, the government realised it had a problem
item.description: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

item.title: Report says growing Sydney's tree canopy would save $1 billion in health costs
item.description: A series of greenways crisscrossing Sydney's west presents an opportunity to grow the city's canopy and make more public space, a Committee for Sydney report suggests.

item.title: Jannik Sinner beats Alexander Zverev to win Wimbledon again
item.description: The top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev for his second consecutive title at Wimbledon after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue.

item.title: SA doctor banned for inappropriate relationship with patient who accused him of 'grooming' her
item.description: A former Adelaide doctor will not be able to reapply for registration for at least two years after he was banned for having an inappropriate relationship with his client.

item.title: 'Like my men's shed': John O'Toole celebrates over 600 matches with Huon Valley Soccer Club
item.description: For more than 30 years, John O'Toole has quietly helped build a local soccer club. On Sunday, he surpassed his 600th match while playing alongside his sons.

item.title: Convicted paedophile argues multiple-personality evidence unlawful in appeal
item.description: A father jailed for abusing two children is appealing against his conviction, arguing evidence given by the multiple personalities of the same witness was unlawful.

item.title: Brendon McCullum fired as England's Test coach but retains white ball duties
item.description: The architect of England's controversial Bazball approach to Test cricket is given his marching orders after a disappointing string of results.

item.title: Why the world's biggest AI companies want data centres in the NT
item.description: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

item.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say
item.description: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

item.title: Cruz Hewitt beaten by American prospect Jordan Lee in Wimbledon boys' final
item.description: Cruz Hewitt comes up short in his bid to win the Wimbledon boys' title, being beaten by Jordan Lee in the final.

item.title: NDIS cuts threaten blind bowler's dream of representing Australia
item.description: A blind bowler from regional WA say his dreams of representing Australia are being swept away along with his NDIS funding.

item.title: Slow Read exhibition asks us to ditch doom-scrolling for art celebrating the printed page
item.description: A new show at Hawthorn Arts Centre offers a range of immersive artworks made from books and paper that demand, and reward, our full attention.

item.title: Proposed amendment to Arapiles Dyurrite plan allows climbing near culturally significant areas
item.description: Climbing Victoria says proposed changes to the Mount Arapiles Dyurrite management plan will be a compromise between climbers and traditional owners built on "trust".

item.title: Gianni Infantino says FIFA will discuss World Cup expansion post-tournament, defends hydration breaks
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino believes this year's World Cup is proof that expanding the tournament was the right move.

item.title: Death toll rises as millions stranded by Bangladesh floods
item.description: Days of torrential monsoon rain have triggered major flooding, leaving millions stranded and facing food supply shortages in the south-east of the country.

item.title: Queensland teen dies, 22 injured in e-mobility device crashes since safety laws brought in
item.description: Regulations introduced in Queensland less than a fortnight ago limit e-mobility devices to 12km/h on footpaths. Riders must be 16 and above or be supervised by a parent. They can also be breathalysed.

item.title: The main culprit of Australia's productivity crisis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bazball is over as Brendon McCullum's deliberately uncomplicated regime falls foul of itself, leaving complicated legacy
item.description: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

item.title: Thomas Tuchel's exchange with Jude Bellingham gives England another chance to prove its World Cup mettle
item.description: This England World Cup squad is proving they are different to their predecessors at every stop. Now their manager has just given them their latest challenge.

item.title: Payne Haas to stay with Samoa and says nation is 'big chance' to win Rugby League World Cup
item.description: Payne Haas has cleared the air about his World Cup plans and says there is a "great chance" Samoa can lift the trophy for the first time.

item.title: Five of our best chicken dinner recipes
item.description: From pot pie to tray bakes and sticky maple wings, here are five of our most popular chicken recipes.

item.title: Rescuers retrieve a sick seabird from Noosa Main Beach
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland wildlife rescuers prepare for bird flu after sick giant petrel found at Noosa
item.description: A northern giant petrel found at Noosa Main Beach is undergoing testing for H5 avian influenza.

item.title: SA coroner calls for changes after man died while paramedic waited outside
item.description: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

item.title: Actor Sam Neill's final take
item.description: Actor Sam Neill might have officially been a Kiwi but he did some of his best work in Australian film and television. At home on either side of the ditch, this is Sam Neill's story.

item.title: Australia has a sperm donor shortage and women are turning to social media and apps
item.description: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

item.title: From Jurassic Park to The Piano, here are Sam Neill's best roles
item.description: With more than 150 acting roles under his belt, Sam Neill leaves a lasting legacy to the film and TV industry, and an array of characters that will stay in fans' hearts forever.

item.title: Geelong forward Jeremy Cameron to have AC joint surgery, Hawthorn's Karl Amon has knee procedure
item.description: Geelong are hopeful star forward Jeremy Cameron will return in time to play a part in their premiership push after suffering a brutal shoulder injury.

item.title: Fiery tobacco wars continue in Melbourne
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Australia facing a sperm donor shortage
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Yemen's Houthis target Saudi airport with ballistic missiles and drones
item.description: The Iran-aligned Houthi movement that controls northern Yemen has accused Saudi Arabia of launching air strikes against the international airport in Sanaa.

item.title: Explain the Internet: Is the controversy over Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey justified?
item.description: The tech trillionaire has spent a good chunk of 2026 yelling on X about how Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey "desecrates" the ancient poet.

item.title: Negotiating your bills could save you thousands. Here's how to start
item.description: Negotiating to reduce your spending and increase your income are tangible ways you can improve your financial situation right now.

item.title: Property developers are benefitting from eased planning rules targeting 'affordable' housing
item.description: State and federal governments are offering incentives for property developers in exchange for more affordable housing. But a Four Corners investigation has uncovered major flaws in the strategy.

item.title: Big super funds and SMSFs could pay compensation for victims of financial misconduct
item.description: The assistant treasurer is looking to revamp funding for the Compensation Scheme of Last Resort, saying that large super funds, as well as people with self-managed super funds (SMSFs), could be asked to chip in.

item.title: Spielberg leads outpouring of global tributes for actor Sam Neill
item.description: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

item.title: More than 2,000 sexual extortion complaints spark warning to young men
item.description: Major online platforms are accused of failing to deploy readily available technology to detect well-known blackmail tactics.

item.title: Video appears to show Thailand pub fire reached flashover 'point of no return', expert says
item.description: A video analysis of an explosive pub fire in Thailand, which killed 30 people, appears to show a flashover "point of no return" that would have made evacuation nearly impossible, an expert says.

item.title: Wannabe Olympic MMA group tried gifting $82,000 cognac to Brisbane Games committee
item.description: The gifts, revealed exclusively by the ABC today, are part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

item.title: Overseas dealers flock to Winton Opal Festival
item.description: The outback town of Winton is known for dinosaur fossils and as the location of the first performance of Waltzing Matilda, but has also become an important part of a growing international luxury trade.

item.title: What happened to Peter Falconio? The outback murder mystery that continues to haunt Australia 25 years on
item.description: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend, Joanne Lees, in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

item.title: Ukraine and allies join to protect Europe from ballistic missiles
item.description: Ukraine and nine other countries will form a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles, utilising Kyiv's experience in fighting Russia's full-scale invasion for over four years.

item.title: Alex de Minaur notches career-high ranking despite Wimbledon exit
item.description: Alex de Minaur's arrow keeps pointing upwards despite his wait for a grand slam title being lengthened by a disappointing Wimbledon exit.

item.title: Will property tax changes stem Australia's productivity crisis?
item.description: With housing prices coming off the boil, maybe our banks will start lending to more productive areas of the economy, like business.

item.title: Dutch referee Rob Dieperink dead at 38 two months after World Cup axing
item.description: Dutch football mourns the sudden death of "highly valued referee" Rob Dieperink just months after FIFA removed him from the panel of World Cup officials.

item.title: Five whale entanglements in five days bring sharks, close beaches on Ningaloo Reef
item.description: Authorities save one animal but another succumbs to its injuries, leading sharks to converge on a popular snorkelling spot.

item.title: Sharks feed on whale carcass off Exmouth
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ICE agents shoot and kill Colombian motorist in Maine
item.description: A federal immigration officer has fatally shot a motorist in Maine, the second time in a week that US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents have used deadly force.

item.title: Mid-East ceasefire in tatters as Trump reinstates blockade, Iran strikes US bases
item.description: US President Donald Trump says the US is reinstating its blockade of Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and will start charging for ships from elsewhere to transit through it.

item.title: Former Spanish PM slammed over 'intolerable whiffs of racism' towards France team
item.description: Mariano Rajoy finds himself in hot water after saying France's team "does not have any French players" ahead of the World Cup semifinal.

item.title: Cameron Munster may miss Melbourne Storm's NRL finals due to knee surgery
item.description: Cameron Munster goes under the knife for knee surgery, likely ruling him out of action for the majority of regular season.

item.title: World Cup: Spain 'here to suffer' and France unites against racism ahead of semifinal
item.description: Spain is invoking the aura of the Roman Empire, while France is lighting up the pitch and remaining united off it, ahead of their men's World Cup semi-final in Dallas.

item.title: The four stages of the mental load
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'I lost a normal childhood': What it's like being a young carer
item.description: Kenny and Naomi were both younger than 10 when they became carers for family members.

item.title: US military releases vision they say show strikes against Iranian targets
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Considerable spike in oil price could spell higher petrol prices for motorists
item.description: The recent closure of the Strait of Hormuz saw the oil price lift, but buying in the market accelerated following Donald Trump's latest Truth Social post.

item.title: Authorities seize nearly $10 million worth of illegal tobacco and vapes in Tasmania
item.description: Tasmanian authorities seized five times as many cigarettes and three times as many vapes in the last financial year as they did the year prior. Enforcement agencies have been given greater powers, including the ability to shut down stores selling illegal tobacco.

item.title: Copyright law is now the biggest battleground in Australia's AI boom
item.description: Tens of billions of dollars in investment, and Australia's ambition to become a global AI power, could hinge on a law written in 1968 for an era of black-and-white television, film and radio.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns extend Jed Walter contract, Western Bulldogs suffer Bailey Dale injury blow
item.description: Shunning big-money offers from rival clubs, Jed Walter said the lure of achieving AFL glory with his childhood club made re-signing a "pretty easy decision".

item.title: Judge rules Donald Trump's settlement with IRS tried to 'manipulate the judicial process'
item.description: US President Donald Trump's lawsuit against his own government over leaked tax records was an unparalleled exercise in self-dealing, a US judge says in a scathing ruling.

item.title: Jarome Luai joins Parramatta, Benji Marshall slams relationship breakdown rumours
item.description: Benji Marshall has slammed "bull**** rumours" claiming a fractured relationship with Jarome Luai contributed to the star five-eighth's early exit from Wests Tigers.

item.title: Mystery surrounds sudden departure of Mildura Base Public Hospital chief executive
item.description: Mystery surrounds the departure of the chief executive of Mildura Base Public Hospital, who led the north-west Victorian hospital through its transition from private to public.

item.title: Stingers see home water polo World Cup as chance to grow own legacy
item.description: Australia's best-performing Paris Olympics team see Sydney's World Cup finals as a way to stay in the conversation outside the Games cycle.

item.title: Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating by Australian Road Assessment Program
item.description: Parts of the Sturt Highway, which connects Sydney to Adelaide and provides an integral part of the country's freight network, have been given the lowest possible safety rating by the Australian Road Assessment Program (AusRAP).

item.title: Cyber warning over Russian hackers targeting critical industries
item.description: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

item.title: Racism remains a key issue for AFL players while Indigenous AFLW players rate workplace culture low
item.description: Racism remains a key issue for First Nations AFL players, while Indigenous AFLW players rate their workplace culture significantly lower than their peers, a new report finds.

item.title: Woman strangled by wheelchair lap belt due to staff shortage, court hears
item.description: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

item.title: NSW scrapped roadworks speed limits outside construction hours. Should the ACT follow?
item.description: The NRMA is encouraging all states and territories to follow the lead of NSW in removing reduced speed limits for roadworks outside construction hours.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Shayna Jack announces swimming retirement on eve of Commonwealth Games
item.description: Gold medal-winning Australian swimmer Shayna Jack says she will retire at the end of the Commonwealth Games.

item.title: ASIC moves to wind up Capital Guard, alleging it pushed fake bonds
item.description: The financial watchdog is seeking to shut down an investment company accused of taking $17 million from 80 victims, including by promoting fake bonds.

item.title: Victorian teachers set to strike again following deadlocked negotiations
item.description: A fresh statewide strike by Victorian public school teachers will he held next week, after negotiations with the Victorian government reached an impasse.

item.title: PBS access to two vital MS treatments under threat
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Launceston General Hospital experiencing 'impacted' capacity due to high demand
item.description: Tasmania's top health official says the Launceston General Hospital is experiencing "impacted" capacity and is urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

item.title: Angus Taylor rules out One Nation coalition once and for all
item.description: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has renewed his criticism of Pauline Hanson's party and categorically ruled out any alliance with it.

item.title: Anthony Albanese maps out AI future with new national framework
item.description: Labor will develop a new national framework in response to artificial intelligence as it seeks to address the technology’s growing economic and social impacts.

item.title: Buffett severs donations to Gates Foundation following Epstein revelations
item.description: The American investor will end his longtime donations to the Microsoft co-founder's non-profit.

item.title: Donald Trump ditches plan for fee in Strait of Hormuz as Iran and US trade new strikes
item.description: Donald Trump reneges on his pledge to charge a 20 per cent fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a day after saying he would introduce it.

item.title: Spain soars into World Cup final with stunning upset over favourite France
item.description: Spain is through to the World Cup final thanks to a 2-0 win over France.

item.title: Art class uses creativity to paint different picture of mental health
item.description: In regional NSW, one program shows that creativity may be the key to better mental health.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 15: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip
item.description: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

item.title: Impressionism's legacy reconsidered in exhibitions at AGSA and Geelong Gallery
item.description: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

item.title: The Thai child sex exploitation scourge unfolding at bars, restaurants and hotels
item.description: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

item.title: Batteries 'cheaper' than gas plants as data centres fuel turbine costs
item.description: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

item.title: Japan ramps up defences amid concerns about China's 'military activities'
item.description: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the midst of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

item.title: Telstra compensation process puts onus on customers, consumer body says
item.description: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

item.title: Australia's highest-paid executive lives in US and earns 500 times that of average full-time worker
item.description: Australia's highest-paid executive collected a salary nearly 500 times that of the country's full-time average worker.

item.title: What would Australia's economy look like under One Nation?
item.description: From interest rates to policy advice from billionaire buddy Gina Rinehart, here is how One Nation's economic platform could look.

item.title: Royal commission hears Jewish student's mental health deteriorated while at Monash University
item.description: Paris Enten has told the antisemitism royal commission her experiences of Monash University, including during an overseas trip, caused her mental health to deteriorate.

item.title: Live: US blockade of Iran begins as explosions rock southern Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he will not charge a 20 per cent toll on traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, as the US military confirms its naval blockade of Iran has come into effect. Follow live.

item.title: Woman sues ex-Catholic Archbishop Julian Porteous over alleged abuse by another priest
item.description: A Tasmanian woman is suing former Archbishop of Hobart Julian Porteous and the state of Tasmania over allegations of sexual abuse by two others when she was a child.

item.title: Buyers 'back in the driver's seat as property market slows in south-east Queensland
item.description: After a decades-long real estate boom, experts say the balance of power is finally shifting from sellers to buyers.

item.title: Counterterrorism police treating suspected murder of Ann Widdecombe as a targeted attack
item.description: After initially saying the alleged murder of British former politician Ann Widdecombe was not politically motivated, police now say they believe it was a targeted attack.

item.title: Fertiliser company Neutrog fined over smelly manure piles at Kanmantoo
item.description: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

item.title: New owner of Rushy Lagoon reveals plans for Tasmania's largest farm
item.description: The buyer of Tasmania's largest farm says in addition to the pine plantation it has planned for the property, it's also considering cropping and leasing out parts of the farm for grazing, tourism and mountain biking.

item.title: Sydney hobby clubs tackling loneliness and creating community
item.description: Hobby-based pop-up clubs are attracting people from university students and office workers to new migrants and academics.

item.title: Two people killed in truck crash at Heathcote, north of Melbourne
item.description: A car and truck collided about 9.45pm on the McIvor Highway at Heathcote.

item.title: Concern for 'vexatious' proceedings allowing residents to shut down Victorian councils
item.description: Advocates say that unproven charges such as those against councillors at Hepburn Shire, triggering automatic stand-down laws, can bring any other council across the state to a shuddering halt.

item.title: Tadej Pogačar wins stage 10 to extend Tour de France lead over rivals
item.description: Tadej Pogačar takes another step towards a fifth Tour de France win with a breakaway stage victory, with some fans booing him because of his dominance.

item.title: AI-powered glasses improve everyday life for disability sector amid privacy concerns
item.description: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

item.title: Kakadu's closed waterfalls spark stern warning from NT tourism minister
item.description: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

item.title: Perth man Cameron Hughes's death in Bali leaves trail of debt, customers in limbo
item.description: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

item.title: Live: Minister pushes back on calls to cut tobacco excise as black market grows
item.description: Clare O'Neil says reducing the tobacco excise to address the rising take-up of illicit tobacco will not help Australians give up smoking. Follow live.

item.title: Australia grapples with child psychiatrist shortage as experts warn of consequences
item.description: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

item.title: NRLW players hope NRL broadcast deal brings longer season, full-time salaries
item.description: A $5.3 billion broadcast deal, booming participation and new contracts on the horizon have NRLW stars hopeful their league can soar to new heights.

item.title: Miners killed decades ago still waiting for recognition
item.description: For decades, families of Mount Isa miners killed on the job have waited for a lasting tribute, but after years of campaigning, some fear their loved ones will be forgotten.

item.title: Live: ASX to follow Wall Street higher as US reimposes naval blockade on Iran
item.description: The Australian share market is likely to rise in morning trade after some weaker-than-expected US inflation figures boosted Wall Street and reduced the likelihood of the Federal Reserve lifting interest rates. Follow live.

item.title: Tasmanian government has 'in-principle' support to explore purchasing Launceston's Boag's Brewery site
item.description: The Tasmanian government wants to buy Launceston's Boag's Brewery site and says it has "in-principle" support from the Japanese parent company to explore the idea.

item.title: Life at Davis Station: 24 minutes of sun after six weeks of darkness
item.description: Researchers spending winter at Australia's southernmost Antarctic research station gathered to celebrate the return of the sun following weeks of darkness.

item.title: Man dies in public place shooting in Carlingford, in Sydney's north-west
item.description: A man has died in a public place shooting in Sydney's north-west.
